back_translation: Then Zophar the Naamathite answered:
'So my anxious thoughts urge me to answer, because of the turmoil within me.
I have heard a rebuke that insults me, and my understanding prompts me to reply.
Do you not know that from ancient times, since man was placed on the earth,
that the joyful shout of the wicked is short, and the gladness of the godless lasts only a moment?
Though his arrogance reaches the heavens and his head touches the clouds,
he will perish forever like his own dung; those who saw him will ask, 'Where is he?'
He will vanish like a dream and never be found; he will be chased away like a vision of the night.
The eye that saw him will see him no more, and his place will behold him no longer.
His children will have to beg favor from the poor, and his own hands must return his wealth.
His bones, full of youthful vigor, will lie down with him in the dust.
Though evil is sweet in his mouth and he hides it under his tongue,
though he cannot bear to let it go and keeps it in his mouth,
yet his food turns sour in his stomach, becoming the venom of cobras within him.
He swallows wealth but vomits it up; God forces it out of his stomach.
He will suck the poison of cobras; the fangs of a viper will kill him.
He will not enjoy the streams, the rivers flowing with honey and cream.
He must return the fruit of his labor without swallowing it; he cannot enjoy the profits of his trade.
For he has oppressed and forsaken the poor; he has seized houses he did not build.
Because his greed is never satisfied, he cannot escape with his treasure.
Nothing is left for him to devour; therefore his prosperity will not endure.
In the fullness of his abundance he will fall into distress, and the full force of misery will come upon him.
When he has filled his stomach, God will vent His fierce wrath upon him and rain it down on him as he eats.
Though he flees from an iron weapon, a bronze-tipped arrow will pierce him through.
The arrow is pulled from his back, the gleaming point from his liver. Terrors come upon him.
Total darkness is reserved for his treasures; a fire no one kindled will consume him and devour what is left in his tent.
The heavens will expose his iniquity, and the earth will rise up against him.
The possessions of his house will be carried off, flowing away on the day of His wrath.
This is the wicked man's portion from God, the inheritance God has appointed for him.'
